title: Trump threatens to attack Iran tonight as ceasefire collapses, sparking oil surge and global market drops
summary: The US president states that the ceasefire with Iran 'has ended' and that he could attack 'tonight', which sends oil prices soaring and causes sharp declines in global stock markets, as investors seek refuge in the dollar.

title: Trump confuses Iran with Japan and refers to the 'Islamic Republic of Japan'
summary: The US president made a verbal mistake by referring to Japan as the 'Islamic Republic of Japan' amid his statements on the situation in the Middle East.

title: NATO summit concludes with commitment to increase defense spending and aid to Ukraine
summary: NATO members reaffirmed collective defense and agreed on a support package for Ukraine worth 13 trillion yen, while President Trump insists that allies should contribute more.

title: Trump authorizes Ukraine to produce Patriot missiles under license to bolster its air defense
summary: The US president grants a license for the production of Patriot missiles in Ukraine, which would allow the country to counter Russian ballistic missile attacks and improve its defense capability.

title: Japanese Parliament begins debate on reform of the Imperial House Law to ensure succession
summary: The government and opposition parties have begun deliberations on the reform aimed at ensuring a sufficient number of imperial family members, although the main opposition party, the CDP, opposes the bill.

title: IMF cuts global growth forecast to 3.0% for 2026 due to rising energy costs
summary: The International Monetary Fund lowered its global GDP estimate by one-tenth of a percentage point, citing rising energy prices and geopolitical tensions as main factors for the slowdown.

title: Footballer Kaoru Mitoma crashes his car into a bicycle, leaving a woman injured
summary: The Japan national team and Brighton player was driving when he collided with a bicycle at an intersection; investigations indicate that both may have run a red light.

title: Toyama Airport changes nickname to 'Toyama-Takayama Sushi Airport' to attract tourists
summary: The new name, which replaces the previous 'Toyama Kito Kito', includes the city of Takayama and has generated mixed opinions, as authorities seek to boost foreign tourism in the region.

title: Japan marks fourth anniversary of former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe's assassination
summary: Political leaders, citizens, and family members remember Abe in various commemorative events, while the need to address the legacy and issues related to the Unification Church is highlighted.

title: Former Tokyo Special Prosecutors Office prosecutor investigated for inappropriate relationship with a suspect
summary: The prosecutor, who was in charge of political corruption cases, allegedly had a romantic relationship with a woman who was the subject of his investigations, leading to a disciplinary file.

title: Payment processor Zenshinshin declares bankruptcy with ¥115.1 billion in debt
summary: The company, which provided services to more than 20,000 businesses in Japan, leaves thousands of establishments unpaid, also affecting regional banks.

title: Typhoon 9, of great intensity, approaches Okinawa islands with maximum winds of 60 m/s
summary: Weather authorities warn of the arrival of Typhoon No. 9 to the Sakishima Islands, where torrential rains and risk of landslides are expected.

title: Corporate bankruptcies in Japan exceed 5,000 in first half of 2026, highest in 12 years
summary: Rising labor costs and inflation hit the restaurant and accommodation sectors hard, pushing bankruptcies up 2.4 times from the previous year.

title: Naomi Osaka reaches Wimbledon quarterfinals, highlights her progress on grass
summary: The former world No. 1, who had struggled on grass, achieves her best result at the London tournament and expresses satisfaction at overcoming her fears.

title: Kyushu Electric subsidiary loses device with customer data, announces new security measures
summary: The incident, which involves the disappearance of an SSD with contractual information, leads the company to restrict the use of external storage devices and strengthen data protection policies.

title: Two former employees of insurer Fukoku Life arrested for defrauding ¥1.25 million in medical insurance
summary: The former sales agents allegedly submitted false claims for compensation to fraudulently collect insurance from the company itself.

title: Yukihiko Inoue, former head of Japan's National Police Agency who led response to Tokyo subway sarin attack, dies
summary: Inoue, 88, also led operations during the shooting of a police chief and during the 1995 Tokyo subway sarin attack, remembered for his leadership in critical moments.

title: Nordic combined excluded from 2030 Winter Olympics program
summary: The IOC's decision to eliminate this discipline, in which Japan has historically performed well, provokes reactions of nostalgia and sadness among Japanese athletes and fans.

title: Midfielder Daichi Kamada renews contract with Crystal Palace until 2027
summary: The Japanese international footballer extends his link with the English Premier League club, where he has regained his best form this season.

title: Japan to create new 'Information and Technology' subject in secondary education with up to 70 class hours per year
summary: The Ministry of Education plans to double the hours dedicated to digital and programming education in high schools to prepare students in the use of artificial intelligence and computer skills.
